User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the Slice & Spice application.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

email.description: The email address of the user.

phone.description: The phone number of the user.

definition.description: Stores user profile information.  Includes the 'role' field for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

definition.description: Indicates whether a user has admin privileges. Existence of a document at this path grants admin access.  Content of document is irrelevant.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who might be an admin.

firestore.reasoning:
This design prioritizes security and scalability for the Slice & Spice application, particularly around user authentication and authorization. It leverages Structural Segregation and Authorization Independence to simplify security rules and enable robust data access control. The key collections are `/users` for user profiles, `/roles_admin` for admin role assignments.  The design emphasizes denormalization to avoid hierarchical `get()` calls in security rules, ensuring atomic operations. Path-based ownership secures user-specific data, while existence checks for admin roles simplify admin authorization.

**Authorization Independence:** The `users` collection stores the `role` property directly within the user document. This eliminates the need for security rules to fetch user roles from a separate location, enabling atomic operations and simplifying rule logic.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
*   The separation of user roles into a distinct collection (`/roles_admin`) and the role property in the user collection enables secure `list` operations. Listing of users can be secured based on their role. Existence checks on `/roles_admin/{userId}` can determine admin privileges.
